diagnosis: Barcode window starts before the inline barcode.
next_action: Scan offsets and rerun with the best-supported start.

diagnosis: The same barcode sequence is assigned to more than one sample.
next_action: Fix the barcode sheet before interpreting sample-level counts.

diagnosis: The observed sequence is within one edit of multiple barcode targets.
next_action: Do not enable one-edit rescue until the barcode library is collision-safe.

diagnosis: The read is compatible with multiple targets under the configured radius.
next_action: Do not rescue ambiguous reads into either sample without changing the barcode design or assignment policy.

diagnosis: The extracted barcode window is low complexity or dominated by N calls.
next_action: Review base quality and upstream sequencing/run quality before assigning.

diagnosis: The nearest one-edit correction is supported only by high-confidence bases.
next_action: Keep the read unmatched unless the quality policy is deliberately changed.

diagnosis: The configured window cannot be extracted from this read.
next_action: Check read length, read side, trimming, and fixed-window coordinates.

diagnosis: The read matches a reverse-complement candidate under a different orientation assumption.
next_action: Normalize barcode orientation in the sheet or assay specification, then rerun.
